interpretation: Fair — significant data quality issues detected

recommended_actions.action: Investigate duplicate records. Add dedup logic keyed on non-key fields.
Fix null values in required column 'tenant_id' at the source.
Investigate root cause of vacancy_loss discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.
Investigate root cause of delinquency discrepancy for 2025-12. Check for duplicate events, missing data, or filter logic differences.

top_risks.business_impact: Duplicate records inflate aggregations and distort metrics
top_risks.title: Near-duplicate rows detected

top_risks.business_impact: Null values in required fields break filters and aggregations
top_risks.title: Null values in required column: tenant_id

top_risks.business_impact: Reported vacancy_loss for 2025-12 is 5.1%, but recomputed value from raw events is 0.0%. Discrepancy of +100.0%.
top_risks.title: vacancy_loss misreported for 2025-12 (off by +100.0%)

top_risks.business_impact: Reported delinquency for 2025-12 is 2.7%, but recomputed value from raw events is 2.9%. Discrepancy of -8.7%.
top_risks.title: delinquency misreported for 2025-12 (off by -8.7%)

executive_summary: BayesIQ audited this real estate firm's portfolio performance reporting across vacancy tracking, tenant delinquency, and collection rates. We found 4 issues — including a complete vacancy loss fabrication (reported 5.1%, actual 0.0%), null tenant IDs breaking lease-level aggregations, and 60 duplicate property records. LP distributions were being calculated on fundamentally incorrect occupancy assumptions. After remediation, the reliability score improved from 43 to 65.
